page: Vultr voc-g-32c-128gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor. The system is configured with 32 shared vCPUs, derived from 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. It includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided by a 640 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates strong multi-core capabilities and high-tier memory performance. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranks in the top 10% with a score of 3103.67, while multi-core performance is in the top 25% at 99997.49. Large-block memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 537.96 GB/sec for read-only operations and 292.8 GB/sec for write operations, both placing in the top 10%. Passmark CPU tests, including encryption and physics, consistently rank in the top 25%, alongside a bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speed of 209.38 GB/sec.

While the instance delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and strong multi-core compute, its shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for workloads requiring sustained, dedicated CPU performance. Database operations and web serving throughput perform in the middle 50% tier, with Redis SET operations achieving a strong 73.66 million operations per second. This hardware profile is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded data processing, medium-scale database hosting, and moderate LLM inference tasks, such as 7B model text generation where it achieves 30.05 tokens per second.
description: Vultr voc-g-32c-128gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance powered by an AMD EPYC x86_64 processor. It features 32 shared vCPUs (16 physical cores, 2 threads per core) running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM and a 640 GB NVMe SSD. The server delivers strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Its memory subsystem is highly efficient, with large-block read and write bandwidth placing in the top 10% at 537.96 GB/sec and 292.8 GB/sec, respectively. While the shared CPU allocation is a consideration for sustained peak workloads, the instance offers balanced resource density. It is highly suited for general-purpose applications, multi-threaded data processing, Redis caching layers, and moderate language model inference.
og_description: Vultr voc-g-32c-128gb-640s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute instance featuring 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 640 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong multi-core and top-tier memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Vultr voc-g-32c-128gb-640s-amd is an AMD EPYC-powered instance with 32 shared vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and a 640 GB NVMe SSD for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced multi-core virtual server featuring high-speed NVMe storage and top-tier memory bandwidth for diverse general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 32 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB of system memory
640 GB local NVMe SSD storage for fast I/O operations
Top-tier large-block memory read and write bandwidth
Strong multi-core CPU and cryptography benchmark performance
Suitable for general-purpose applications, database hosting, and caching
